title: perilla frutescens var. japonica
intro: Perilla frutescens var. japonica, also known as Shiso or Japanese perilla, is a plant native to East Asia and a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. The health benefits of Perilla frutescens var. japonica include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves are used fresh in salads or as a garnish, while its seeds and roots are used in cooking and as a condiment.
